source_title: "Do airlines charge solo travelers more? Some deal experts says yes."
pricing_terms_used: dynamic pricing, dynamic pricing algorithms, real-time booking data, fare buckets, surge pricing, pricing strategy
information_tracked: "real-time and historic booking data, demand, distance, itinerary, cabin class, number of travelers, solo traveler status"
summary: The Washington Post reports on Thrifty Traveler's finding that Delta, United, and American appeared to show higher one-way fares for some solo travelers than for groups on identical flights. For United, the article cites a Chicago O'Hare to Peoria example where a one-person fare was higher than the per-person fare for two or more travelers. The piece frames the practice as part of airline dynamic pricing and fare-bucket management, notes that United did not respond to requests for comment, and includes outside experts debating whether the pattern is new or a form of business-traveler targeting.
key_excerpts: "Potter's team at Thrifty Traveler, a travel deal and news site, looked at hundreds of fares this week and concluded that Delta, United Airlines and American Airlines were listing higher ticket prices on some routes for solo passengers than for a pair or group fares." | "In one example, they found that a one-person fare from Chicago O'Hare to Peoria, Illinois, on United was $269, while the price dropped to $181 per person when booked for two or more passengers." | "United and American did not respond to requests for comment."

source_title: "Exclusive: US Airlines Are Quietly Hitting Solo & Biz Travelers with Higher Fares"
pricing_terms_used: pricing tactic, pricing dynamic, fare buckets, fare classes, segmenting, higher fares, price points
information_tracked: "number of passengers, fare class availability, accompaniment restriction, solo traveler status, presumed business traveler status, corporate card use"
summary: Thrifty Traveler says its analysts found examples where United, American, and Delta charged some solo travelers more than groups of two or more on the same one-way domestic flights. The article gives United examples from Chicago O'Hare to Peoria and Chicago O'Hare to Lexington, arguing that United opened cheaper fare buckets only when the search included multiple passengers. It attributes the likely rationale to customer segmentation aimed at less price-sensitive business travelers and states that United did not respond to a request for comment.
key_excerpts: "All three of the country's largest carriers (American Airlines, United Airlines, and Delta) are penalizing solo travelers with higher ticket prices than you can book when traveling with a group." | "When searching for one passenger, the lowest economy price is a Q economy fare - United's 'discount coach' fare. But by searching for multiple travelers, you can pull in (even cheaper) S class fares, which United considers a 'deep discount coach' ticket." | "No one from American, Delta, or United responded to requests for comment from Thrifty Traveler on this pricing strategy."

source_title: "Navan Enhances Direct Connection with United Airlines As TMC Introduces NextGen Corporate Booking Experience"
pricing_terms_used: continuous pricing, more price points, heightened personalization, optimal pricing, dynamically display, tailored deals, customized shopping experiences
information_tracked: "company policy, personal preferences, loyalty status, MileagePlus Premier status, ancillary product eligibility"
summary: Navan and United announce an enhanced direct connection for corporate travel booking, saying the integration gives customers more fare options, 40% more price points, United continuous pricing, and personalized booking displays. Navan says its platform uses AI to incorporate company policy, personal preferences, and optimal pricing to dynamically display relevant booking options. United's quoted sales executive frames the integration as a way to provide access to United offers, ancillary products, and MileagePlus Premier recognition, not as an allegation of individualized consumer price discrimination.
key_excerpts: "Featuring United's continuous pricing, Navan leverages AI to incorporate company policy, personal preferences, and optimal pricing to dynamically display the most relevant booking options to customers." | "Key benefits of the United direct connection include: Increased price points and savings: United Airlines NDC content offers 40% more price points than traditional EDIFACT distribution channels and increased fare options, driving program savings." | "Through this integration, we're excited to provide Navan customers access to the full range of United offers including all ancillary products and recognition of Mileage Plus Premier status."

source_title: "Sabre testing live NDC offers with United Airlines and Beyond NDC Agency Partners"
pricing_terms_used: NDC offers, new fare options, personalized fares, revenue management, offer management
information_tracked: "traveler expectations, booking channel, fare options, ancillary amenities, servicing needs"
summary: Sabre announces live testing of NDC offers with United as launch carrier and says the APIs enable NDC bookings, more robust shopping, new fare options, and additional flight amenities. United's vice president of revenue management says the partnership improves the flight-shopping and booking process for customers. A quoted American Express Global Business Travel executive links NDC access to personalized fares and servicing in an omnichannel environment, so the source is useful industry context for United's distribution and offer-management strategy.
key_excerpts: "Sabre's NDC solutions will allow United to offer new fare options and additional flight amenities for a more comprehensive shopping experience for customers who choose to book through Sabre." | "United is thrilled to be the first airline to partner with Sabre to offer NDC capabilities through their booking platform as we continue to advance the travel industry." | "Modern business travelers have specific expectations: they want access to all content and they want to know they're getting the best personalized fares and servicing in an omnichannel environment."

source_title: "Score bonus miles: Register now for United's latest Mile Play promotion"
pricing_terms_used: targeted promotions, targeted members, offers vary, different rewards and requirements, fare threshold
information_tracked: "MileagePlus number, MileagePlus status, member eligibility, trip count, fare amount, booking and travel dates"
summary: The Points Guy describes United's Mile Play promotion, in which targeted MileagePlus members receive different bonus-mile offers and requirements. Examples from TPG staff show offers varying by member, status, number of required trips, bonus-mile amount, and minimum fare thresholds. The source concerns personalized promotional incentives and fare conditions rather than base airfare pricing, and it does not include a United comment beyond instructions to use United's registration page.
key_excerpts: "United Airlines has launched a new round of Mile Play promotions, which award bonus miles to targeted members after they complete specific tasks." | "As is normal with these promotions, offers vary widely from one member to another." | "United may not have targeted some members for Mile Play, while others will see different rewards and requirements to earn them."

source_title: "United Launches Airline Industry's First Media Network"
pricing_terms_used: personalized advertising, personalized real-time offers, targeted advertising, anonymized audience segments, personalized experiences
information_tracked: "travel behaviors, United customer data, U.S. customers aged 18 and over, aggregated and anonymized audience segments, mobile app engagement, inflight entertainment engagement, MileagePlus membership"
summary: United announces Kinective Media, an airline media network that uses travel-behavior insights to connect advertisers with customers through United channels. The release says advertisers use aggregated and anonymized audience segments rather than personally identifiable information, and says U.S. customers can opt out of targeted advertising. Although the source is about advertising rather than ticket prices, it documents United's use of customer data for personalized real-time offers and audience segmentation.
key_excerpts: "Kinective Media by United Airlines is the only media network that uses insights from travel behaviors to connect customers to personalized advertising, experiences and offers from leading brands." | "Advertising technology platform uses United's data to create anonymized audience segments that marketers can reach on the airline's mobile app, inflight entertainment screens and more." | "Advertisers cannot access the personally identifiable information of United customers - instead, Kinective Media leverages the insights of U.S. customers aged 18 and over to create aggregated and anonymized audience segments."
